sections.paragraph:
χώρα, ας, ἡ [cp. χῆτος ‘want, lack’ and associated terms such as χήρα ‘one who is bereft’, χῆρος ‘bereaved’, χωρίς ‘without’; ‘open space’]

—1. ‘a stretch of territory as contrasted with owned property’, defined by national or place names or narrative context

—a. region, area Mt 8:28; Mk 1:5; 5:1, 10; Lk 2:8; 3:1; 15:13-15; Ac 12:20; 13:49; 16:6; 26:20. Open country contrasted w. city J 11:55; Ac 8:1. Shoreline contrasted with sea 27:27.

—b. homeland Mt 2:12.

—c. in transf. sense Mt 4:16.

—2. ‘area under a proprietor’, landed property Lk 12:16; pl. fields 21:21; J 4:35; Js 5:4.
